observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

effect the necessary correcting entries to correct the erroneous recording using the following pro-forma entry:
submit to the office of the Resident Auditor the copy of the JEV for the corrections made; and
moving forward, evaluate properly every repair or improvement done on the properties of the LGU so it will be correctly recorded in the books.

We recommended that the BAC strictly follow the guidelines on resorting to Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the appropriate mode of procurement was employed.

We recommended that management submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and the Report of Official Travels as prescribed by the abovementioned guidelines to facilitate the validation of the reasonableness of gasoline consumption of the Municipality. Also, direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure the submission of complete DTTs with the required serial number and completely filled out with necessary information for every trip undertaken.

We recommended that management:

require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the inspection activity;
direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and 
direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
review and to determine the source agency of those funds entrusted to the LGU for proper recording of these accounts in the Inter-Agency or Intra Agency Payable account;
validate and verify the existence of the claims by coordinating the claimant and to gather the perfected contract or any document covering such liability; and
revert those accounts with no valid claimants and submit the copy of the journal entry voucher to the Office of the Auditor.

We reiterated our recommendation that management should:
require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees by withholding or suspension of their salaries; and
moving forward, require the attachment of the Accountant’s Certification and strictly implement that no additional cash advance shall be granted to officer or employee unless previous cash advances given to him are fully settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entry to adjust the erroneous booking of PPE accounts taking into consideration the capitalization threshold of P15,000 for recognition as PPE as provided in Section 4 of the Circular;
require the Municipal Accountant to submit the copy of the Journal Entry Voucher of the correcting entry; and
direct the Property Custodian to issue Inventory Custodian Slip to the end users.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the acquisition of any biological assets at fair value less cost to sell in accordance to the provisions of the PPSAS to reflect the gain or loss on the initial recognition of the purchased biological assets;
Direct the Project-in-Charge/Municipal Agriculturist to submit the inventory of the oil palms seedling still held by the LGU as of December 31, 2021 by group according to significant attribute that is used in the market as the basis for pricing, for example, by age or quality. Submit the inventory to Municipal Accountant for proper recording of gain or loss, if any and for reconciliation.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the biological assets account to reflect the reliable valuation in accordance to PPSAS No. 27 upon receipt of the inventory with fair market values and costs to sell.
Direct the Project-in-Charge to account any biological transformation of the oil palms such as growth, degeneration, and reproduction as well as separate records on the agricultural produce of the said biological assets with corresponding fair values and costs to sell and report any changes periodically to the Municipal Accountant to update the books of the LGU.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers reflecting the adjustments of value of the biological assets caused by biological transformations, if any.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide further disclosures related to Biological Assets held by the LGU as required under the provisions of the PPSAS.

9.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly ensure that all expenditures for procurement of goods are duly supported with the necessary documents before processing and certifying the DVs; and henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

10.6 We recommended that the Property Custodian regularly renew/update the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is a change in custody of accountability to strengthen control and accountability over the government assets.

11.6 We recommended that the municipal assessor update the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any, and to regularly conduct real property assessment every three (3) years in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management
Require the concerned department heads to prepare and submit its AAPSI concerning the audit observations and recommendations stated in LGU's ARR for CY 2020;
Moving forward, require all department heads to prepare and submit AAPSI for the following years using the prescribed form shown under COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014; and
Designate an employee who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.

13.7 We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and trainings to capacitate them in the execution of GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

16.3 We recommended that the GFPS recommends to the SB the passing of the ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, the ordinance shall include provisions on the following: measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; imposition of fines; and creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

We recommended that the accountable officer concerned comply strictly with existing regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid penalties and comply with the provisions of the RR No. 1-87 and 2-98.

We recommended that the Property Custodian regularly renew/update the PARs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is a change in custody of accountability to strengthen control and accountability over the government assets.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the DRRM-MOOE and savings from DRRM-CO to the Trust Liability-DRRM account as required by the COA Circular No. 2012-002 provisions to avoid misuse of the said fund and to program this amount in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that the transactions affecting these accounts are recorded properly, and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

We recommended that management 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reverse the entries made to restate the receivables account that has been closed to the Government Equity account; and
Gather the necessary documents as supporting papers and immediately file for request for write off these receivables with the COA ATL and/or SA.

We recommended that the management:
direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to regularly prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks to be submitted to the Accounting Office for their reference; and
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV for the restoration of the unreleased checks in the books of accounts of the LGU every yearend

We recommended that management 

direct municipal health office and other offices that are requesting for drugs and medicines to base their requests on the PNDF and submit together with the purchase request the required certification;
direct the Bids and Awards Committee, in addition to the documents required under RA 9184, to include all the documents required under Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 as part of the documentary requirements in the bidding of drugs and medicines; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to allow processing of the claim for delivered drugs and medicines only if supported with complete documentary evidence.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) within the prescribed period of 24 hours after the receipt, and acceptance of the consumable items and after the inspection has been conducted by the LGU Inspector of the items received.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit RCDs and RCIs to the Municipal Accountant in a regular basis so that the latter could also prepare the journal entry vouchers to be submitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
We recommended that management instruct the GSO to have the vehicles of the Municipality marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the department/office where such vehicle has been issued.

We recommended that management prepare the Report on Monthly Utilization of BGCM and the Status of Implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format from the date it was received by the LGU until to date, submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor, and post the same to the three designated public conspicuous places of the Municipality with proper and visible identification.

We recommended that management refrain from charging to LDRRMF expenditures which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop payment of expenditures not related to LDRRMF programs and activities.
We recommended that the management must monitor the status of unsettled receivables and demand or enforce collection from concerned liable persons/entities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the details and amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, Annex D.

We recommended that management to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the Municipality. Moreover, require the personnel concerned to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

We recommended that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. Further, management to monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.
We recommended that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.

We recommended that management:
require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries; and
moving forward, require the attachment of the Accountant’s Certification and strictly implement that no additional cash advance shall be granted to officer or employee unless previous cash advances granted to him are fully settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that the transactions affecting these accounts are recorded properly, and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

We recommended that management
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reverse the entries made to restate the receivables account that has been closed to the Government Equity account; and
Gather the necessary documents as supporting papers and immediately file for request for write off these receivables with the COA ATL and/or SA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
effect the necessary correcting entries to correct the erroneous recording using the following pro-forma entry:
submit to the office of the Resident Auditor the copy of the JEV for the corrections made; and
moving forward, evaluate properly every repairs or improvements done on the properties of the LGU so it will be correctly recorded in the books.

We recommended that the management:
direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to regularly prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks to be submitted to the Accounting Office for their reference; and
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV for the restoration of the unreleased checks in the books of accounts of the LGU every yearend.

We recommended that the BAC strictly follow the guidelines on resorting to Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the appropriate mode of procurement was employed.

We recommended that management submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and the Report of Official Travels as prescribed by the abovementioned guidelines to facilitate the validation of the reasonableness of gasoline consumption of the Municipality. Also, direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure the submission of complete DTTs with the required serial number and completely filled out with necessary information for every trip undertaken.

We recommended that management:
require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the inspection activity;
direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

We recommended that management
direct municipal health office and other offices that are requesting for drugs and medicines to base their requests on the PNDF and submit together with the purchase request the required certification;
direct the Bids and Awards Committee, in addition to the documents required under RA 9184, to include all the documents required under Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 as part of the documentary requirements in the bidding of drugs and medicines; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to allow processing of the claim for delivered drugs and medicines only if supported with complete documentary evidence.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) within the prescribed period of 24 hours after the receipt, and acceptance of the consumable items and after the inspection has been conducted by the LGU Inspector of the items received.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit RCDs and RCIs to the Municipal Accountant in a regular basis so that the latter could also prepare the journal entry vouchers to be submitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
We recommended that management instruct the GSO to have the vehicles of the Municipality marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the department/office where such vehicle has been issued.

We recommended that management prepare the Report on Monthly Utilization of BGCM and the Status of Implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format from the date it was received by the LGU until to date, submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor, and post the same to the three designated public conspicuous places of the Municipality with proper and visible identification.

We recommended that management refrain from charging to LDRRMF expenditures which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop payment of expenditures not related to LDRRMF programs and activities.

We recommended that management shall include the list of programs, projects and activities to be charged to unexpended balances of LDDRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities.
We recommended that LDRRMO to strictly comply with the rules on submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that official concerned should comply with the aforementioned COA Circular to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the job order contracts and contracts of services of the LGU.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to Item C.8 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for the preparation and further guidelines on the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report. Likewise, submit the lacking pertinent reports/documents to be attached to the submitted GAD AR of your LGU.
We recommended that management shall ensure that HGDG tool specifically the PIMME GAD checklist-Box 16 and 17 are administered to the identified GAD PPAs in the GPB during the preparation of GAD AR.

We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines.

We recommended the following courses of action:

The Municipal Mayor to direct the appropriate office to inform the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;

The appropriate office to prepare and submit a monthly monitoring report on project status; and

The Municipal Engineering Office to monitor and evaluate the maintenance of project status in the project signboard as current as possible and updated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the management must revert to the General Fund the LDRRMF balances in the Trust Fund account which remained unutilized for five years from the date of transfer to be made available for implementation of programs, projects or activities for other social services.

We recommended that the management to:
Clearly indicate in the contract/job order the services/jobs of the personnel to be hired as well as their expected output; and

Identify the nature of the duties and responsibilities of the personnel in order to determine the proper account where their salaries/wages are to be accounted.

We recommended that the accountable officer concerned comply strictly with existing regulation on the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid penalties and comply with the provisions of the RR No. 1-87 and 2-98.

We recommended that management:

to conduct capacity development in order to orient the GFPS of their functions, duties and responsibilities, and other LGU employees about GAD and related topics; and

to conduct gender analysis in order to identify the real gender issue of the locality to be addressed.

We recommended that management conduct monitoring on the status of bid securities in order to return these to the respective bidders or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the management conduct monitoring on the status of performance securities in order to return these balances to the respective bidders or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that management comply with the reporting guidelines of COA Circular No. 2015-008 that the total road network system shall be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445. Further, furnish this office with JEV for the adjustment made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made and render timely audit and information to management on the results of the audit. Failure on the part of the officers and employees concerned to submit the required documents and financial report shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended the following courses of action:  

Comply and implement Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 with subject:  “Demand for Immediate Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances Outstanding as of December 31, 2011; and

Conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the cash advances which are outstanding and dormant for ten years or more for possible write-off taking into consideration the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 and the preparation of the documents to support the request for authority to write-off from the COA.
We recommended that management conduct verification, analysis and validation of the receivables and existence of the concerned debtors to enforce collection.

We recommended the following courses of action:

The Municipal Mayor to direct the appropriate office to inform the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted;

The appropriate office to prepare and submit a monthly monitoring report on project status; and

The Municipal Engineering Office to monitor and evaluate the maintenance of project status in the project signboard as current as possible and updated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the details and amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, Annex D.

We recommended that the management must revert to the General Fund the LDRRMF balances in the Trust Fund account which remained unutilized for five years from the date of transfer to be made available for implementation of programs, projects or activities for other social services.

We recommended that the accountable officer concerned comply strictly with existing regulation on the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid penalties and comply with the provisions of the RR No. 1-87 and 2-98.

We recommended that management:
to conduct capacity development in order to orient the GFPS of their functions, duties and responsibilities, and other LGU employees about GAD and related topics;

to conduct gender analysis in order to identify the real gender issue of the locality to be addressed; and

revisit PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 or Guidelines on the Localization of Magna Carta for Women

We recommended that management conduct monitoring on the status of bid securities in order to return these to the respective bidders or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the management conduct monitoring on the status of performance securities in order to return these balances to the respective bidders or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445. Further, furnish this office with JEV for the adjustment made.

We recommended that management to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the Municipality. Moreover, require the personnel concerned to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

We recommended that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. Further, management to monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of LDRRM funds in accordance with Paragraphs 5.1 to 5.4 of Section 5 of the JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG and approve only the procurement of items falling within those enumerated in Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 of the DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made and render timely audit and information to management on the results of the audit. Failure on the part of the officers and employees concerned to submit the required documents and financial report shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries.
We recommended that the management must monitor the status of unsettled receivables and demand or enforce collection from concerned liable persons/entities.

We recommended that management shall include the list of programs, projects and activities to be charged to unexpended balances of LDDRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities.
We recommended that LDRRMO to strictly comply with the rules on submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that official concerned should comply with the aforementioned COA Circular to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the job order contracts and contracts of services of the LGU.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to Item C.8 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for the preparation and further guidelines on the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report. Likewise, submit the lacking pertinent reports/documents to be attached to the submitted GAD AR of your LGU.
We recommended that management shall ensure that HGDG tool specifically the PIMME GAD checklist-Box 16 and 17 are administered to the identified GAD PPAs in the GPB during the preparation of GAD AR.

We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that management:

Prioritize the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and

Analyse the reconciling items in the PPE account and prepare correcting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to immediately make a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise due care in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions. A copy of the JEV taking up the adjustments shall be furnished to the Office of the Auditor

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer:

Issue an Executive Order creating the CPES – IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrating the CPES function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES – IU in accordance with CPES Implementing Guidelines for Infrastructure Projects; and

Formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines.

We recommended that management shall establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system and that includes the Local Chief Executive to issue Administrative Order creating the GAD M&E Team as required under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Human Resources Management Office and the Office of the Municipal Accountant must refrain from granting monetization of leave credits in excess of 30 days in a given year except in meritorious cases as provided under Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave with complete documentary requirements.

We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 5,000.00 as prescribed by the law. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as stated in the General Appropriations Act.

We recommended that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables.  This way, the actual RPT/SET Receivables every beginning of each year is reflected in the books of accounts. Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

Require the strict compliance in allocating 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that the LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of Children can adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.
We recommended that Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles and protect from the natural elements to prolong its useful life

We recommended that management should not authorize deductions from the salaries of employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445. Further, furnish this office with JEV for the adjustment made.
observations.reason: The payables are still outstanding and unpaid at the end of CY 2018. Verification of claimants in order to settle the outstanding payables was not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the Municipality. Moreover, require the personnel concerned to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.
observations.reason: Original documents on the land acquisition (i.e. Deed of sale) cannot be located which is required in the titling procedures.

observations.recommendation: Require the strict compliance in allocating 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that the LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of Children can adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.
observations.reason: The appropriated amount for the year 2018 still failed to meet the requirement of 1% of the LGU’s IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles and protect from the natural elements to prolong its useful life
observations.reason: At the end of FY 2018, still the LGU has no garage to shelter their vehicles however, they have budgeted an amount, in their 2019 Annual Budget, for the construction of garage.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. Further, management to monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.
observations.reason: All dormant account totaling ₱111,777.16 were still not reverted back to the General Fund, Source Agency or Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:


           a. Require all officials and employees to liquidate  their  cash
              advances immediately;


           b.  Institute  appropriate  legal  sanction  against   concerned
              officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their
              salaries;

           c. Comply and implement Items 6.0 and 7.0 of  COA  Circular  No.
              2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 with subject:   “Demand  for
              Immediate Liquidation and Settlement  of  All  Cash  Advances
              Outstanding as of December 31, 2011; and

           d.  Conduct  verification,  analysis  and  validation   of   the
              existence of the cash  advances  which  are  outstanding  and
              dormant for ten years or more for possible  write-off  taking
              into consideration the provisions of  COA  Circular  2016-005
              and the preparation of the documents to support  the  request
              for authority to write-off from the COA.

We recommended that management:


        a. Prioritize the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the  accounting
           records; and


        b. Analyse the reconciling items in the  PPE  account  and  prepare
           correcting journal entries to reflect the  correct  balances  of
           the affected accounts.

We recommended that management conduct verification,  analysis  and
   validation of the receivables and existence of the concerned  debtors  to
   enforce collection.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal  Accountant  to
   immediately  make  a  detailed  review  of  the  accounts  with  abnormal
   balances, determine the cause/s of the same,  and  effect  the  necessary
   adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present  the  correct
   balances of the affected accounts.   Henceforth,  exercise  due  care  in
   recording,  analyzing  and  classifying  the   Municipality’s   financial
   transactions. A copy of the  JEV  taking  up  the  adjustments  shall  be
   furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We reiterate our recommendation that management, in accordance with Item 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 to a) suspend the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the agency, who failed to liquidate their cash advances that have been outstanding for (1) year and over as of December 31, 2017 and b) request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances of those accountable officers who had already died.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already issued demand letters for liquidation of outstanding cash advances and is in the process of analyzing the accounts to be requested for authority to write-off.
observations.reason: Despite the issuance of demand letters, most of the advance remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Accountant should make a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, abnormal balances on various accounts amounting to P639,187.81 remained uncorrected or unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance representing the balance of the 30% Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE in the GF bank account to the Special Trust Fund account.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification in the expense accounts affected and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriate and sufficient appropriations for various expense accounts, to ensure fair presentation of the said accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of government funds in the release of financial assistance to NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables.  This way, the actual RPT/SET Receivables every beginning of each year is reflected in the books of accounts.  Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, the RPT and SET Receivables still has a difference amounting to P12,412.02.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should not authorize deductions from the salaries of employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 4,000.00.  Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.
observations.reason: The Accountant failed to consider the new threshold provided under GAA of FY 2018, thus there are still a number of employees with net take home pay below the prescribed amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Finance Committee adopt appropriate measures to control not only the appropriations/ allotment, but the cash availability as well.  The Municipal Treasurer shall, in certifying that funds are available per Box B of the DV and preparing checks for any disbursement, ensure that indeed funds are available for the purpose to preclude the incurrence of cash deficit.
We recommend that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that management, in accordance with Item 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 to suspend the salary of the accountable officers who are still employed in the agency, who failed to liquidate their cash advances that have been outstanding for (1) year and over as of December 31, 2017 and to request for authority to  write-off the unliquidated cash advances of those accountable officers who had already died.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Accountant should make a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise care in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts once verified and found to be without valid claims, as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445. Further, furnish this office with Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the adjustment made.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance representing the balance of the 30% Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE in the General Fund bank account to the Special Trust Fund account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification in the expense accounts affected and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriate and sufficient appropriations for various expense accounts, to ensure fair presentation of the said accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of government funds in the release of financial assistance to NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the Municipality. Moreover, require the personnel concerned to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables.  This way, the actual RPT/SET Receivables every beginning of each year is reflected in the books of accounts. Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
Require the strict compliance in allocating 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that the LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of Children can adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles and protect from the natural elements to prolong its useful life.

Recommendation
We recommend that management should not authorize deductions from the salaries of employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. Further, management to monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Finance Committee (LFC) adopt appropriate measures to control not only the appropriations/allotment, but the cash availability as well.  The Municipal Treasurer shall, in certifying that funds are available per Box B of the disbursement voucher (DV) and preparing checks for any disbursement, ensure that indeed funds are available for the purpose to preclude the incurrence of cash deficit.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Development Council (MDC), in the preparation of the AIP and to facilitate its implementation, identify the specific projects to be implemented otherwise, management obtain the approval of the sanggunian prior to its disbursements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that:
The MBO, MPDO, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to review the unobligated appropriations of completed PAPs under the 20% EDF and the availability of such fund balances in the depository bank; and
After determining the fund balances in the bank, and in order to maximize the utilization of related funds, consider the following courses of action:
Revert the unobligated/unexpended appropriations of completed projects for re-appropriation to other priority projects;
Require the MPDO to revisit the unfunded priority projects in the development plan or propose new projects for the consideration thereof in the re-appropriation of the unappropriated surplus;
Request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass the related resolutions on the budget to be funded from the unappropriated surplus for the specific priority projects for implementation; and
Execute without delay such projects which are determined to still address the present development needs of the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of LDRRM funds in accordance with Paragraphs 5.1 to 5.4 of Section 5 of the JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG and approve only the procurement of items falling within those enumerated in Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 of the DILG.

We recommend that   the Members of Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all inventories held by the Municipality and submit a properly accomplished Report of the Physical Count of Inventories every semester starting CY 2017 and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation and adjustment of the related inventory accounts. It is also recommended that the Inventory Committee ensure compliance by the property custodians on the submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued for consolidation to facilitate the recording of expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend the conduct of an annual inventory of all PPE under the Special Education Fund to ensure fairness of accounts’ presentation in the financial statements. The RPCPPE to be prepared and submitted on a timely basis. The report to be properly filled up; ensuring that all descriptions in the columns and items presented are reported and accounted for. The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian/Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required stock cards, equipment ledger cards and property cards respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that   the Municipal Mayor direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made and render timely audit and information to management on the results of the audit. Failure on the part of the officers and employees concerned to submit the required documents and financial report shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Strategies and measures are done to fast tract the submission of reports and necessary documents within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Upon segregation and filing of the DVs and reports in the preparation for submission, the lack of supporting documents in some vouchers delays the submission to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) to refrain from charging the regular MOOE of various schools to the Special Education Fund. Instead, allocate or use such fund for the construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, acquisition of books and periodicals and sports development which cannot be charged against the downloaded MOOE of the schools. Also, the LSB to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund and the Accountant to charge payments of services rendered by teachers under Job Order appointment which are not related to the establishment of extension classes. Henceforth, the SEF should be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided for under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: Require the strict compliance of the budgetary limitation as provided for under Sec. 325 (a) of R.A. 7160 to ensure that the LGU shall have a balance budget and that funds are allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects. Consider coming up with other sources of income to cope up with the PS limitations.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management to re-examine and update the Local Revenue Code and/or amend existing ordinances in accordance with the Local Government Code and require the Local Finance Committee to consider on what provisions of the Revenue Code that needs to be retained, deleted and added to have sound projections of the LGUs financial requirements. Further, updating and rationalizing the tax rates of the Revenue Code once every five years as required under Section 191 of the Local Government Code helps to ensure the sustainability and resilience of various local taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee already deliberated the proposal on the update and revision of the Local Government Code and they are now in the process of publishing the updated Local Revenue Code.
observations.reason: The deliberation made by Local Finance Committee was done near the end of CY 2017 already.

observations.recommendation: The MPDC and other personnel concerned to prepare at-least a three-year Capability Development (CapDev) Agenda of the LGU as mandated under DILG MC 2007-71; and refrain from attending/conducting trainings/activities not included in the CapDev to maximize the Officials and employees capability and potentials and to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month for verification and validation on its utilization. Further, require the responsible officers to adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.
observations.managementAction: Management is working out plans and programs to implement the project as soon as possible
observations.reason: Due to remoteness of project location which resulted to a difficulty in transporting the construction materials to the project area

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month for verification and validation on its utilization. Further, require the responsible officers to adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management Complied with the Recommendat ion

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the copies of the Bank Statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements submitted to the Office of the Auditor for the verification process.

We recommend that the General Service Officer should prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption supported by all of the Driver’s Trip Ticket to provide the team with adequate documentation in evaluating the regularity and reasonableness of the fuel consumption incurred by the Municipality.

We recommend that the Accountant, before effecting payment on the claims, should see to it that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers duly provided with relevant details.

We recommend that the General Service Officer should prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption supported by all of the Driver’s Trip Ticket to provide the team with adequate documentation in evaluating the regularity and reasonableness of the fuel consumption incurred by the Municipality.

We recommend that the Accountant, before effecting payment on the claims, should see to it that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers duly provided with relevant details
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office Makes sures that the required reports are attached on disbursement vouchers upon the processing of payment to suppliers.
observations.reason: Separate Reports and attachment on supporting Documents were not submitted to the Audit team on a monthly basis for in the timely evaluation on the reasonableness of the fuel consumption of the Municipality.. Such documents only reach the Office of the Audit Team Leader only when the vouchers are received and subject for post audit.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
Management officials through the leadership of the LCE should be commended for their prompt action to comply with RA 9003.  Likewise, additional funding for programs, projects and activities that are related to solid waste management should be appropriated for the continuous improvement of the Municipal Solid Waste Management.

Recommendation:
Continue the implementation of projects that promote and contribute to the economic development of the LGU and possibly act as role model for other LGUs to follow for the development of the countryside and the entire country as a whole.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Members of Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all inventories held by the Municipality and submit a properly accomplished Report of the Physical Count of Inventories every semester starting CY 2017 and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation and adjustment of the related inventory accounts. It is also recommended that the Inventory Committee ensure compliance by the property custodians on the submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued for consolidation to facilitate the recording of expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommend the conduct of an annual inventory of all PPE under the Special Education Fund to ensure fairness of accounts’ presentation in the financial statements. The RPCPPE to be prepared and submitted on a timely basis. The report to be properly filled up; ensuring that all descriptions in the columns and items presented are reported and accounted for. The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian/Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required stock cards, equipment ledger cards and property cards respectively.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management, in accordance with Item 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 to suspend the salary of the accountable officers, if still employed in the agency, who failed to liquidate their cash advances that have been outstanding for (1) year and over as of December 31, 2016 and to request for authority to write-off for those accountable officers who are already dead.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) to refrain from charging the regular MOOE of various schools to the Special Education Fund. Instead, allocate or use such fund for the construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, acquisition of books and periodicals and sports development which cannot be charged against the downloaded MOOE of the schools. Also, the LSB to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

Recommendation:
We recommend to the Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund and the Accountant to charge payments of services rendered by teachers under Job Order appointment which are not related to the establishment of extension classes. Henceforth, the SEF should be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided for under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.

Recommendation:
Require the strict compliance of the budgetary limitation as provided for under Sec. 325 (a) of  R.A. 7160 to ensure that the LGU shall have a balance budget and that funds are allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects. Consider coming up with other sources of income to cope up with the PS limitations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to revert the cited Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees amounting to ₱537,074.63 and ₱82,664.67, respectively as required in Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and furnish this office with Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the adjustment made.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification in the expense accounts affected and the City Budget Office to provide appropriate and sufficient appropriations and allotment for the various expense accounts, to ensure fair presentation of the said accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made and render timely audit and information to management on the results of the audit. Failure on the part of the officers and employees concerned to submit the required documents and financial report shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management to re-examine and update the Local Revenue Code and/or amend existing ordinances in accordance with the Local Government Code and require the Local Finance Committee to consider on what provisions of the Revenue Code that needs to be retained, deleted and added to have sound projections of the LGUs financial requirements. Further, updating and rationalizing the tax rates of the Revenue Code once every five years as required under Section 191 of the Local Government Code helps to ensure the sustainability and resilience of various local taxes.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management to create a committee composed of members from the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Assessor with primary objective of retrieving prior years’ documents and reconciliation of records to determine the realistic balance of receivables from Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes so that necessary adjustment(s) in the books of accounts can be effected to provide accurate balance of receivable accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
The MPDC and other personnel concerned to prepare  at-least a three-year Capability Development (CapDev) Agenda of the LGU as mandated under DILG MC 2007-71; and refrain from attending/conducting trainings/activities not included in the CapDev to maximize the Officials and employees capability and potentials and to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:
Require the strict compliance in allocating 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that the LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of Children can adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.

Recommendation:
Require the strict compliance in allocating PPAs that can be attributed and built in within the budget levels for Senior Citizens, Persons with Disabilities, and AIDS and properly follow the procedures in the Local Budget Preparations as provided in the annual Local Budget Memoranda for every calendar year.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects that are surely workable and implementable during the year.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month for verification and validation on its utilization. Further, require the responsible officers to adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should cause the cancellation of the stale checks and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for fair presentation of the cash in bank and payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The agency complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the copies of the Bank Statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements submitted to the Office of the Auditor for the verification process.
observations.managementAction: Still on the process of verifying the reconciling items. We promised to make necessary adjustments on the books of accounts once the reconciling items have been verified.
observations.reason: Management is having a hard time verifying the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Engineer to display notification so that the public will be properly informed in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The agency complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the General Service Officer and Municipal Engineer to submit copies of government contracts and purchase orders including Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required in COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.managementAction: The agency complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government Unit Human Resource Management Officer require all Job Orders and Casuals to post daily/promptly their attendance in the Logbook of attendance or DTR upon arrival and departure from office and to submit their Accomplishment Reports together with their DTRs.
observations.managementAction: The agency complied with the recommendation
